---
title: "Marker-based annotation evidence"
subtitle: "Differential markers and broad marker signature overlap"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
.warning-list {
  border-left: 4px solid #c2410c;
  background: #fff7ed;
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  margin: 0.7rem 0 1rem;
}
.warning-list p {
  margin: 0.35rem 0;
}
.note-box {
  border-left: 4px solid #2563eb;
  background: #eff6ff;
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  margin: 0.7rem 0 1rem;
}
.compact-table-note {
  color: #475569;
  font-size: 0.92rem;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable {
  width: auto !important;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable td {
  white-space: normal;
  width: auto !important;
  max-width: 28rem;
  vertical-align: top;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable td.dt-nowrap,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-nowrap {
  white-space: nowrap;
}
.dataTables_wrapper .dataTables_scrollBody {
  overflow-x: auto !important;
}
</style>
```

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
from html import escape
import json
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import HTML, display

try:
    import plotly.express as px
except ImportError:
    px = None

try:
    from itables import show
except ImportError:
    show = None

provider_dir = Path(".")
payload = json.loads((provider_dir / "annotation_result.json").read_text())
markers = pd.read_csv(provider_dir / "tables" / "differential_markers.csv")
signatures = pd.read_csv(provider_dir / "tables" / "marker_signatures.csv")
strength = pd.read_csv(provider_dir / "tables" / "marker_strength_summary.csv")
run_input = payload.get("input", {})
status = payload.get("status", {})
methods = payload.get("methods", [])
cluster_predictions = payload.get("cluster_predictions", [])
warnings = status.get("warnings", [])

if show is not None:
    import itables.options as itable_options

    itable_options.showIndex = False
    itable_options.classes = "display compact stripe hover"
    itable_options.layout = {
        "topStart": "pageLength",
        "topEnd": "search",
        "bottomStart": "info",
        "bottomEnd": "paging",
    }
    itable_options.maxBytes = "1MB"

PLOT_TEMPLATE = "plotly_white"
STRENGTH_COLORS = {"strong": "#1b9e77", "moderate": "#d95f02", "weak": "#7570b3"}

def empty_to_unknown(value):
    return value if value not in {"", None} else "not provided"

def clip_text(value, limit=110):
    text = "" if pd.isna(value) else str(value)
    return text if len(text) <= limit else text[: limit - 1] + "..."

def compact_table(df, columns, n=50, text_columns=()):
    available = [column for column in columns if column in df.columns]
    if not available:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)
    out = df.loc[:, available].head(n).copy()
    for column in text_columns:
        if column in out.columns:
            out[column] = out[column].map(clip_text)
    return out.reset_index(drop=True)

def display_static_table(df):
    display(HTML(df.reset_index(drop=True).to_html(index=False, escape=False)))

def display_interactive_table(df, *, caption=None, page_length=10, order=None):
    df = df.reset_index(drop=True)
    if show is None:
        display_static_table(df)
        return
    show(
        df,
        caption=caption,
        showIndex=False,
        scrollX=True,
        column_filters="footer",
        pageLength=page_length,
        lengthMenu=[10, 25, 50, 100],
        order=order or [],
        autoWidth=True,
        autoWidthMode="fit",
        deferRender=True,
        classes="display compact stripe hover",
    )

def candidate_rows(max_candidates=3):
    rows = []
    for pred in cluster_predictions:
        cluster_id = str(pred.get("cluster_id", ""))
        candidates = pred.get("candidates", [])
        if not candidates:
            rows.append(
                {
                    "cluster": cluster_id,
                    "rank": "",
                    "candidate": "no marker signature match",
                    "confidence": "unknown",
                    "score": "",
                    "matched genes": "",
                    "top markers": "",
                }
            )
            continue
        for candidate in candidates[:max_candidates]:
            evidence = candidate.get("evidence", {})
            rows.append(
                {
                    "cluster": cluster_id,
                    "rank": candidate.get("rank", ""),
                    "candidate": candidate.get("label_raw", ""),
                    "confidence": candidate.get("confidence_bucket", ""),
                    "score": candidate.get("provider_score", ""),
                    "matched genes": ", ".join(evidence.get("matched_genes", [])),
                    "top markers": ", ".join(str(item.get("gene", "")) for item in evidence.get("top_markers", [])[:8]),
                }
            )
    return pd.DataFrame(rows).reset_index(drop=True)

def plot_unavailable(reason):
    display(HTML(f"<div class='note-box'>{escape(reason)}</div>"))

context_mode = "context-light" if empty_to_unknown(run_input.get("tissue")) == "not provided" else "context-aware"
```

## Summary

This report is provider-level evidence for reviewing cluster identities. It is not a final cell
type assignment. The provider first ranks cluster markers with `scanpy.tl.rank_genes_groups`. For
human datasets, it also compares informative markers with broad built-in human marker signatures.
For non-human organisms such as zebrafish, human signature scoring is skipped unless a
species-specific or explicit ortholog-mapped catalog is added.

```{python}
summary_table = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"field": "provider state", "value": status.get("state", "unknown")},
        {"field": "context mode", "value": context_mode},
        {"field": "input h5ad", "value": run_input.get("h5ad", "")},
        {"field": "cluster key", "value": run_input.get("cluster_key", "")},
        {"field": "organism", "value": empty_to_unknown(run_input.get("organism"))},
        {"field": "tissue", "value": empty_to_unknown(run_input.get("tissue"))},
        {"field": "gene id type", "value": empty_to_unknown(run_input.get("gene_id_type"))},
        {"field": "expression layer", "value": empty_to_unknown(run_input.get("expression_layer"))},
        {"field": "clusters reported", "value": len(cluster_predictions)},
    ]
)
display_static_table(summary_table)
```

If tissue is not provided, the report remains useful for exploration, but all labels should be
treated as context-light hypotheses. Tissue-specific marker databases, references, or atlas models
should be configured explicitly before making final biological claims.

## Warnings

```{python}
if warnings:
    warning_html = "<div class='warning-list'>" + "".join(
        f"<p><strong>Warning {index}.</strong> {escape(str(warning))}</p>"
        for index, warning in enumerate(warnings, start=1)
    ) + "</div>"
else:
    warning_html = "<div class='note-box'>No provider warnings were recorded.</div>"
display(HTML(warning_html))
```

## Cluster Review

Use this table as the first manual review surface. It is searchable, sortable, filterable, and
paginated. Long gene lists are shortened here so the table stays usable; the full CSV artifacts are
kept in `tables/`.

```{python}
review = candidate_rows(max_candidates=3)
for column in ["matched genes", "top markers"]:
    if column in review:
        review[column] = review[column].map(lambda value: clip_text(value, 120))
display_interactive_table(review, caption="Candidate labels by cluster", page_length=10, order=[[0, "asc"], [1, "asc"]])
```

## Figures

### Marker Strength by Cluster

Strong and moderate markers are more useful for manual review than weak markers. A cluster with few
informative markers may need re-clustering, QC review, or a different annotation method.

```{python}
if px is None:
    plot_unavailable("Plotly is not available, so the marker-strength figure was not rendered.")
elif len(strength):
    long_strength = strength.melt(
        id_vars=["cluster_id", "n_cells", "n_markers"],
        value_vars=["strong", "moderate", "weak"],
        var_name="marker_strength",
        value_name="marker_count",
    )
    fig = px.bar(
        long_strength,
        x="cluster_id",
        y="marker_count",
        color="marker_strength",
        color_discrete_map=STRENGTH_COLORS,
        hover_data=["n_cells", "n_markers"],
        category_orders={"marker_strength": ["strong", "moderate", "weak"]},
        labels={
            "cluster_id": "Cluster",
            "marker_count": "Number of ranked markers",
            "marker_strength": "Marker strength",
            "n_cells": "Cells",
            "n_markers": "Ranked markers",
        },
        title="Ranked marker strength per cluster",
        template=PLOT_TEMPLATE,
    )
    fig.update_layout(height=460, legend_title_text="Strength", bargap=0.22)
    fig.update_xaxes(type="category")
    fig.show()
else:
    plot_unavailable("No marker strength table was produced.")
```

### Signature Score Heatmap

This heatmap helps distinguish strong broad-lineage patterns from weak scattered hits. It shows the
top signature matches per cluster, with darker color indicating stronger heuristic overlap.

```{python}
if px is None:
    plot_unavailable("Plotly is not available, so the signature heatmap was not rendered.")
elif len(signatures):
    top = signatures.query("rank <= 5").copy()
    matrix = top.pivot_table(index="label", columns="cluster_id", values="score", aggfunc="max", fill_value=0)
    matrix = matrix.loc[matrix.max(axis=1).sort_values(ascending=False).index]
    fig = px.imshow(
        matrix,
        aspect="auto",
        color_continuous_scale="Viridis",
        labels={"x": "Cluster", "y": "Candidate label", "color": "Score"},
        title="Marker-signature overlap score",
        template=PLOT_TEMPLATE,
    )
    fig.update_layout(height=max(420, 28 * len(matrix.index)), margin={"l": 120, "r": 30, "t": 70, "b": 50})
    fig.show()
else:
    plot_unavailable("No built-in marker signatures matched the differential markers.")
```

### Top Marker Evidence

This compact plot shows the strongest few marker genes per cluster. Color tracks adjusted p-value
and point size tracks log2 fold change. The complete marker list is better reviewed in the
searchable table below.

```{python}
if px is None:
    plot_unavailable("Plotly is not available, so the marker evidence figure was not rendered.")
elif len(markers):
    marker_plot = markers.copy()
    marker_plot["pval_adj_num"] = pd.to_numeric(marker_plot["pval_adj"], errors="coerce")
    marker_plot["log2fc_num"] = pd.to_numeric(marker_plot["log2fc"], errors="coerce")
    marker_plot = marker_plot.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).groupby("cluster_id").head(5)
    marker_plot = marker_plot.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).reset_index(drop=True)
    marker_plot["neg_log10_padj"] = marker_plot["pval_adj_num"].clip(lower=1e-300).map(lambda value: -np.log10(value) if pd.notna(value) else None)
    marker_plot["log2fc_size"] = marker_plot["log2fc_num"].clip(lower=0)
    marker_plot["marker"] = marker_plot["gene"].map(str)
    marker_plot["cluster_marker"] = marker_plot.apply(lambda row: f"C{row['cluster_id']} | {row['marker']}", axis=1)
    marker_plot["cluster_marker"] = pd.Categorical(
        marker_plot["cluster_marker"],
        categories=list(reversed(marker_plot["cluster_marker"])),
        ordered=True,
    )
    fig = px.scatter(
        marker_plot,
        x="log2fc_num",
        y="cluster_marker",
        size="log2fc_size",
        color="neg_log10_padj",
        hover_data={
            "cluster_id": True,
            "gene": True,
            "rank": True,
            "score": ":.3f",
            "log2fc": ":.3f",
            "pval_adj": True,
            "strength": True,
            "cluster_marker": False,
            "log2fc_size": False,
            "log2fc_num": False,
            "pval_adj_num": False,
            "marker": False,
        },
        labels={
            "log2fc_num": "log2 fold change",
            "cluster_marker": "Cluster | marker gene",
            "log2fc_size": "log2FC",
            "neg_log10_padj": "-log10 adjusted p-value",
        },
        title="Top marker evidence per cluster",
        template=PLOT_TEMPLATE,
    )
    plot_height = min(760, max(420, 24 * len(marker_plot) + 120))
    fig.update_layout(height=plot_height, margin={"l": 155, "r": 30, "t": 70, "b": 50})
    fig.show()
else:
    plot_unavailable("No differential markers were produced.")
```

## Evidence Tables

Tables in this section are searchable, sortable, filterable, and paginated. For long tables, use the
search box or column filters first; then export or inspect the full CSV files under `tables/` when
you need every row.

### Top Signature Matches

```{python}
signature_table = compact_table(
    signatures,
    [
        "cluster_id",
        "rank",
        "label",
        "score",
        "confidence_bucket",
        "n_matched",
        "coverage",
        "jaccard",
        "matched_genes",
        "signature_species",
        "signature_scope",
    ],
    n=200,
    text_columns=["matched_genes", "signature_species", "signature_scope"],
)
display_interactive_table(signature_table, caption="Top marker-signature matches", page_length=10, order=[[0, "asc"], [1, "asc"]])
```

### Differential Markers

The table below shows the highest-ranked marker genes per cluster. It is capped for report
performance; the full CSV is available in `tables/differential_markers.csv`.

```{python}
marker_table = compact_table(
    markers,
    ["cluster_id", "rank", "gene", "score", "log2fc", "pval_adj", "strength"],
    n=500,
)
display_interactive_table(marker_table, caption="Differential markers", page_length=25, order=[[0, "asc"], [1, "asc"]])
```

## Method Details

```{python}
method_table = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "step": method.get("step", ""),
            "tool": method.get("tool", ""),
            "parameters": json.dumps(method.get("parameters", {}), sort_keys=True),
            "interpretation": method.get("interpretation", ""),
        }
        for method in methods
    ]
)
display_interactive_table(method_table, caption="Provider methods", page_length=10)
```

The marker-signature score is computed as:

```text
score = 0.65 * coverage + 0.35 * jaccard
```

where coverage is the fraction of genes from a built-in signature recovered among informative
cluster markers, and Jaccard is the overlap between informative cluster markers and the signature.
This weighting favors recovering known marker signatures while still penalizing overly broad
matches.

## Interpretation Guidance

- Treat high-confidence matches as candidate labels for review, not as final annotations.
- Review matched genes and the top differential markers together.
- If tissue is unknown, keep broad lineage labels and defer fine labels until a tissue-specific
  reference, marker database, or expert review is available.
- For zebrafish or other non-human organisms, use species-specific marker databases or an explicit
  ortholog-mapped marker catalog. Human built-in signatures are not automatically applied across
  species.
- Compare this report with future reference-based, foundation-model, and manual-curated providers in
  `scrna_audit` before freezing final labels.

## References

- Wolf FA, Angerer P, Theis FJ. 2018. [SCANPY: large-scale single-cell gene expression data analysis](https://genomebiology.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s13059-017-1382-0). *Genome Biology*.
- Heumos L et al. [Single-cell best practices: cell type annotation](https://www.sc-best-practices.org/cellular_structure/annotation.html).
